affected_groups: Parents seeking early academic-track choice for their children
The governing coalition and future governments bearing reversal risk
Pupils currently in or entering 6/8-year gimnázium tracks
content.decision_question: Institutional pluralism and parental/school choice versus centrally engineered equity through structural uniformity
content.evidence_boundary: Contested and partly unknown: the Polish 18-year reform's eventual reversal is a documented fact, but whether it generalizes to the Hungarian coalition context is a judgment call; the effectiveness of anti-sorting enforcement mechanisms in a Hungarian setting is untested and explicitly acknowledged as unproven in the scenario record itself
content.tension: Abolishing the 6/8-year gimnázium entry points via top-down structural reform erases institutional pluralism, church/foundation maintainer autonomy, and parental choice, and carries high political-reversal risk as shown by Poland's 18-year reform being later reversed.
content.title: Decision tension A9: S3
value_poles: centrally engineered equity through structural uniformity
